questions.question: Who is the poet of 'On Killing a Tree'?
questions.explanation: Gieve Patel, an Indian poet and painter, wrote this poem highlighting the strength and resilience of trees.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the poem?
questions.correct_answer: The resilience of nature and the effort required to destroy it
questions.explanation: The poem emphasizes that killing a tree is not easy; it requires removing roots and destroying its life force.

questions.question: How does the poet describe the process of killing a tree?
questions.correct_answer: Cutting, hacking, and chopping alone are insufficient; the roots must be uprooted to truly kill it
questions.explanation: The poet shows that trees are resilient and will regrow unless the root is removed completely.

questions.question: What imagery is used in the poem?
questions.correct_answer: Visual and tactile imagery like bleeding bark, curled twigs, white wet roots, and leprous hide
questions.explanation: Patel uses vivid imagery to convey the tree’s physical resilience and life force.

questions.question: What does the 'root' symbolize in the poem?
questions.correct_answer: The source of life, strength, and resilience
questions.explanation: The root represents the hidden core that sustains the tree, emphasizing that destruction of nature is not simple.

questions.question: Which literary device is used in 'Bleeding bark will heal'?
questions.explanation: The tree is given human qualities, suggesting it can recover and resist harm.

questions.question: What lesson does the poem convey about nature?
questions.correct_answer: Nature is strong and resilient; harming it requires effort, and humans should respect it
questions.explanation: The poem encourages readers to appreciate the strength and persistence of trees and nature.

questions.question: How does the poet describe the tree’s response to harm?
questions.correct_answer: It will sprout new twigs and leaves even after being chopped
questions.explanation: The poet shows the tree’s regenerative power, highlighting nature’s resilience.

questions.question: What is the tone of the poem?
questions.explanation: The poet uses a serious tone to instruct the reader on the effort required to destroy nature and its resilience.

questions.question: What does the poem suggest about human interference with nature?
questions.correct_answer: Humans underestimate the resilience of nature; destroying it is not simple
questions.explanation: The poem warns against careless or thoughtless harm to the natural world.

notes.title: Resilience of Nature
notes.content: Trees can withstand human attacks and will regrow unless their roots are removed.

notes.title: Personification
notes.content: The poet gives human qualities to the tree to emphasize its strength and ability to heal.

notes.title: Imagery
notes.content: Patel uses visual, tactile, and symbolic imagery to show the tree’s life and struggle.

notes.title: Moral Lesson
notes.content: The poem teaches respect for nature and awareness of its strength and endurance.

notes.title: Tone
notes.content: Serious and reflective, highlighting the importance of understanding and respecting natural life.

facts.fact: The poet, Gieve Patel, is also a painter and a practicing doctor.
The poem highlights that killing a tree is difficult and requires effort beyond just cutting its trunk.
It was first published in the 1960s and is part of environmental and modern Indian poetry studies.
The poem uses symbolism and personification to convey the life and resilience of trees.
